---
title: "Classical Bridges"
---

This page is the reader-facing bridge between Circle Calculus and the established Erdos-style combinatorics lanes in the proof-backed showcase. It exists to make the hard-math advertisement auditable without forcing readers to start in the theorem index.

::: {.warning-box}
These are proof-backed bridges to established mathematics, not new proofs of the imported theorems and not progress claims on open Erdos problems. This page is not proof by itself; a theorem card below is Lean-proved only when the generated theorem data and local Lean build support that status.
:::

## Why This Page Matters

Circle Calculus should earn attention by showing that it can express structures mathematicians already care about, then show where the Circle presentation adds inspection value. The current hard-math bridge set covers five lanes:

- zero-sum and sumset structure on cyclic groups,
- Katona's circle method and Erdos-Ko-Rado,
- Roth-style three-term progression structure,
- Hales-Jewett and Ramsey-style unavoidable lines, and
- circulant graph geometry.

Read these as standard-math parity demonstrations first. The Circle-native value is the audit trail: finite-circle vocabulary, examples in Python sidecars, theorem ids, papers, and explicit boundaries.

## Bridge Map

| Showcase | Standard Anchor | Circle Presentation | Boundary |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a data-showcase-ref="SHOW-001" href="../../showcase.qmd#SHOW-001">SHOW-001</a> | EGZ and Cauchy-Davenport | zero-sum witnesses and prime-circle sumsets over `C_n` and `C_p` | no new EGZ or Cauchy-Davenport proof |
| <a data-showcase-ref="SHOW-002" href="../../showcase.qmd#SHOW-002">SHOW-002</a> | Katona and Erdos-Ko-Rado | cyclic-order counting atoms and intersecting-family handles | no new EKR proof or extremal classification |
| <a data-showcase-ref="SHOW-003" href="../../showcase.qmd#SHOW-003">SHOW-003</a> | Roth and three-term progressions | finite witness search tied to formal Roth handles | no improved bounds or cyclic transfer theorem |
| <a data-showcase-ref="SHOW-004" href="../../showcase.qmd#SHOW-004">SHOW-004</a> | Hales-Jewett and Ramsey structure | finite coloring examples tied to formal unavoidable-line theorems | finite searches are examples only |
| <a data-showcase-ref="SHOW-005" href="../../showcase.qmd#SHOW-005">SHOW-005</a> | cycle, circulant, connected, and unit-distance graph vocabulary | cyclic graph models with checked graph handles | no unit-distance or distinct-distance progress claim |

## What To Check

For each lane, verify the same evidence chain:

1. Open the theorem cards below.
2. Open the cited paper.
3. Check the executable sidecar named in the showcase.
4. Confirm the `not_claimed` boundary before repeating the claim.

This page is useful only because that chain stays intact.

## Zero-Sum And Sumset Circles

<p data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_01_ZERO_SUM_CIRCLES.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_01_ZERO_SUM_CIRCLES.md">Zero-Sum Circles</a></p>

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0062"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0063"></div>

## Katona And Erdos-Ko-Rado

<p data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_02_KATONA_EKR_CIRCLE_METHOD.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_02_KATONA_EKR_CIRCLE_METHOD.md">Katona and Erdos-Ko-Rado</a></p>

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0064"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0065"></div>

## Roth And Three-Term Progressions

<p data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_03_ROTH_THREE_AP_CIRCLES.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_03_ROTH_THREE_AP_CIRCLES.md">Roth and Three-Term Progressions</a></p>

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0066"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0067"></div>

## Ramsey Lines And Hales-Jewett

<p data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_04_HALES_JEWETT_RAMSEY_CIRCLES.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_04_HALES_JEWETT_RAMSEY_CIRCLES.md">Hales-Jewett and Ramsey Lines</a></p>

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0068"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0069"></div>

## Circulant Graph Geometry

<p data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_05_UNIT_DISTANCE_CIRCULANT_GRAPHS.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_05_UNIT_DISTANCE_CIRCULANT_GRAPHS.md">Unit-Distance Circulant Graphs</a></p>

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0070"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0071"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0072"></div>

## Vocabulary

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0001"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0201"></div>

::: {.checkpoint-box}
Before citing this page, name one standard theorem, one Circle expression, and one boundary that prevents overclaiming.
:::

<div class="book-navigation">
<a href="index.qmd">Previous: Application Overview</a>
<a href="ai.qmd">Next: AI</a>
</div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
